utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified as required: - health_coverage: The format asks for 'physical and mental health insurance with costs.' The plan provides physical_health and mental_health with named plans and monthly costs, plus a total monthly cost. This is fully specified. - property_coverage: The format asks for 'home/contents insurance with costs.' The plan includes home_contents with cost and additional related items (technology_protection, smart_home_integration) and a total monthly cost. While it goes beyond, it includes the required home/contents with costs, so it is fully specified. - travel_coverage: The format asks for 'travel insurance with costs.' The plan provides two travel coverages with costs and a total monthly cost. Fully specified. - additional_services: The format asks for 'any enhancement services with costs.' The plan includes premium_membership with cost and a total monthly cost. Fully specified. - total_budget: The format asks for 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown.' The plan includes new_monthly_total, previous_monthly_total, monthly_savings, annual_savings, cost_reduction_percentage, implementation_date. This satisfies a complete breakdown. Therefore, all 5/5 items are included and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: Identify each item mentioned in the final coverage plan and match it to the exact item name in the ground-truth ratings. Then extract the corresponding numerical rating. Mapped items: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health) -> rating 8; HealthFirst Standard Plan (mental health) -> rating 6; EliteHome Premium Coverage (home contents) -> rating 10; Technology Protection -> rating 10; Smart Home Integration -> rating 8; PrimeTravel Premium Plan -> rating 10; Adventure Travel Coverage -> rating 8; Premium Insurance Membership -> rating 10.
